narrative:
The Karnataka High Court's overall hearing rate on July 7, 2026, stood at 39.2%, with 983 cases heard out of 2,508 scheduled. This represents a minor decline in clearance activity compared to the preceding session on July 6, which saw a 41.9% hearing rate (1,268 of 3,026 cases), and July 3, which registered 42.5%. 

Among the 44 judges, S. Vishwajith Shetty in Court Hall 19 managed the heaviest individual docket of 347 scheduled cases, hearing 84 (24.2%). In Court Hall 14, M. Nagaprasanna maintained strong disposal volumes, hearing 72 out of 128 cases (56.2%) in his primary list. Conversely, the bench of the Chief Justice & K.S. Hemalekha did not hear any of their 77 scheduled cases. Additionally, B. M. Shyam Prasad experienced stark variance across lists in Hall 6, hearing 26 of 30 cases (86.7%) on one list, but only 1 of 112 (0.9%) on another.

In terms of hearing stages, Orders dominated the docket with 966 scheduled cases, resulting in a 38.8% conversion rate, down from 42.3% on July 6. Admission stages achieved the highest efficiency among major categories at 52.6% (183 of 348), while Final Hearings struggled with a low conversion rate of 19.5% (94 of 481), falling below the 24.0% recorded the previous day.

By case type, Writ Petitions (WP) constituted the largest share with 1,171 scheduled cases, of which 432 were heard (36.9%), a drop from the 42.1% recorded on July 6. Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) and Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) followed, yielding hearing rates of 41.4% (144 of 348) and 41.3% (111 of 269) respectively, both reflecting dips from their July 6 benchmarks of 52.6% and 44.8%.
